mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, generating sustained earnings growth expectations and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive monthly equity returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of observations during mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Subdued quarterly GDP growth indicates stagnating aggregate demand and weak corporate revenue trajectories, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and generating negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns exceed zero in > 50% of cases when GDP growth remains below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or increasing policy rates erodes real discount rates and compresses profit margins, creating a stagflationary pressure that drives negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS yields positive 60-day returns in > 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls growth signals robust labor demand and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and corporate earnings revisions, producing positive monthly S&P 500 returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases fail to generate a positive return bias (mean return < 0) over 20-day windows during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production levels indicate active factory output and supply chain velocity, which correlates with near-term earnings momentum and generates positive weekly S&P 500 returns through momentum trading flows.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or weekly returns are negative in > 50% of high-INDPRO observations.
